title: astragalus layneae
intro: Astragalus layneae, also known as Layne's milkvetch, is a perennial plant in the legume family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing the severity of colds and flu, improving cardiovascular health, and enhancing overall immune function. Astragalus layneae is often prepared as a dried root or herb for herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures, making it a popular supplement among those seeking to boost their immune system and overall well-being.
